headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, billed as its first Mythos-class model, dominates this cycle as educators benchmark it, price it, and wire it into agents.
body: The recommended channels converge on Claude Fable 5: Better Stack frames it as Anthropic's first Mythos-class model topping SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code [8], Cole Medin live tests it against Claude Opus 4.8 to weigh its 2x price premium [1], and Cyril Imhof covers regulated-industry use cases plus a "Know Your Agent" context-engineering method for long-running apps [9]. Agent tooling is the second thread: Cyril Imhof details agent loops, recursive self-improvement, and memory systems [0], while Cole Medin walks through Google's open-source Agents CLI for production deployment with identity management, sandboxed execution, and audit trails [5]. On infrastructure, Groq founder Jonathan Ross discusses LLM-inference hardware, a $20 billion NVIDIA agreement, and Jevons Paradox driving compute demand [2], and NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 series pairs hybrid Mamba transformers with Latent Mixture of Experts [4]. IBM covers AI-accelerated zero-day detection [3] and whether AI agents can be socially engineered, plus a self-replicating AI worm [7].

cited_items.title: Building with Anthropic's Fable 5 LIVE - Is It Really Worth the Hype?
